era: Seattle, 2010s-present. Indie electronic project. Synth-driven, retro-influenced electronic pop. Self-released. Vintage synthesizer aesthetics through modern production.

location.formative: Seattle's electronic music community. Bedroom production. Self-released via Bandcamp and streaming.
location.career: Gold Casio -- Seattle indie electronic project. Self-released. Retro-synthetic shimmer. Vintage synth tones through modern production. E=4.

associated_musicians.name: ODESZA
associated_musicians.note: Cinematic, festival-scale electronic. Opposite end of Seattle electronic from Gold Casio's bedroom retro.

associated_musicians.name: STRFKR
associated_musicians.note: Both Indie Electronic. STRFKR is driving synth-pop pulse. Gold Casio is retro-synthetic shimmer. Same genre, different architectures.

associated_musicians.name: The Postal Service
associated_musicians.note: Pioneered indie-electronic crossover. Gold Casio inherits the tradition, now self-released.

associated_musicians.name: NAVVI
associated_musicians.note: Both shimmer-oriented. NAVVI uses reverb; Gold Casio uses vintage synths. Different tools, similar haze.

associated_musicians.name: Erik Blood
associated_musicians.note: Both Seattle electronic producers. Erik Blood is experimental; Gold Casio is retro-focused.

theory_nodes.concept: The retro-synthetic shimmer (ninety-ninth acoustic element -- the sonic quality of vintage synthesizer tones reinterpreted through modern production; warm analog pads, arpeggiated sequences, retro drum machines, shimmering reverb; nostalgia as frequency; 1980s aesthetics through 2020s sensibility; not reproduction but reinterpretation)
examples: Gold Casio (degree 98) -- vintage synth tones through modern production. Warm, hazy, wistful.
Contrast with the algorithmic tenderness (degree 97, Postal Service electronic) -- intimate, glitchy, digital. The retro-synthetic shimmer is warm, flowing, analog-referenced.
Contrast with the synth-pop pulse (degree 96, STRFKR) -- driving, joyful. The retro-synthetic shimmer is contemplative, hazy.
Contrast with the euphoric build (degree 95, ODESZA) -- cinematic, festival-scale. The retro-synthetic shimmer is bedroom-scale, nostalgic.
theory_nodes.description: The retro-synthetic shimmer is the 99th acoustic element. Vintage synths through modern production. Nostalgia made audible. Warm, hazy, wistful.

ancestors: The algorithmic tenderness (degree 97, Postal Service electronic) -- digital intimacy. The retro-synthetic shimmer is analog-referenced warmth.
The synth-pop pulse (degree 96, STRFKR) -- driving groove. The retro-synthetic shimmer is contemplative.
genealogy.relationship: Ninety-nine acoustic elements across ninety-eight degrees. Track element 100 at degree 99.

theory_nodes.concept: The self-released synthesist (ninety-ninth artist-city pattern -- solo electronic producer operating entirely outside the label system; bedroom-to-Bandcamp pipeline; zero intermediaries; purest DIY expression in the electronic era; logical endpoint of the DIY spectrum)
examples: Gold Casio (degree 98) -- entirely self-released. No label, no management. Bedroom to Bandcamp.
DIY spectrum: ODESZA (95, Counter Records) to STRFKR (96, Polyvinyl) to Postal Service (86/97, Sub Pop) to Gold Casio (98, self-released). Decreasing infrastructure, increasing autonomy.
Seattle's electronic ecosystem supports the self-released model through community, audience, and cultural legitimacy.
theory_nodes.description: The self-released synthesist is the 99th artist-city pattern. Zero intermediaries. Bedroom production. Purest DIY in the electronic era.

ancestors: The re-contextualized return (degree 97, Postal Service electronic) -- about analytical framing. The self-released synthesist is about production and distribution.
The DIY maximalist (degree 96, STRFKR) -- DIY with indie label. The self-released synthesist eliminates even the label.
genealogy.relationship: Ninety-nine artist-city patterns across ninety-eight degrees. Track pattern 100 at degree 99.

theory_nodes.concept: Indie Electronic at Genre Stage 80 (MILESTONE) -- 80th genre stage; third consecutive Indie Electronic (96, 97, 98); same genre, three different sonic architectures; genre label as container, acoustic elements as content
examples: Gold Casio (degree 98) -- Indie Electronic at Genre Stage 80. Milestone!
Three consecutive Indie Electronic: STRFKR (96, synth-pop pulse), Postal Service electronic (97, algorithmic tenderness), Gold Casio (98, retro-synthetic shimmer).
80th genre stage in 98 degrees. Approximately 82% genre density.
theory_nodes.description: Genre Stage 80 -- MILESTONE. Third consecutive Indie Electronic. Same genre, three architectures.

ancestors: Indie Electronic (degree 97, Postal Service electronic) at Genre Stage 79. Third consecutive.
Indie Electronic (degree 96, STRFKR) at Genre Stage 78. The Indie Electronic run begins.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 80 at degree 98. Track at 99.

theory_nodes.concept: E=4 at degree 98 -- self-released bedroom electronic; local following; Bandcamp and streaming; E=4 for consistent quality and dedicated audience without arena-scale reach
examples: Gold Casio (degree 98) -- E=4. Self-released. Local following.
E=4 sustained at degrees 97-98. Consistent electronic energy tier.
Self-released but quality production -- E=4 reflects the music's quality and audience engagement, not distribution scale.
theory_nodes.description: E=4 at degree 98. Self-released. Dedicated audience. Consistent quality.

ancestors: E=4 at degree 97 (Postal Service electronic). E=4 sustained.

cross_references.relationship: ELECTRONIC SPECTRUM. ODESZA is cinematic, festival-scale electronic (E=5, Counter Records). Gold Casio is retro, bedroom-scale electronic (E=4, self-released). Both Seattle electronic; vastly different scales.
INDIE ELECTRONIC PARALLEL. Both Indie Electronic. STRFKR is synth-pop pulse (driving, Polyvinyl). Gold Casio is retro-synthetic shimmer (warm, self-released). Same genre, different architectures.
ELECTRONIC GENEALOGY. Postal Service pioneered indie-electronic crossover on Sub Pop. Gold Casio inherits the tradition, now entirely self-released.
ATMOSPHERIC PARALLEL. Both shimmer-oriented. NAVVI uses reverb-as-architecture. Gold Casio uses vintage synths as atmosphere. Different tools, similar haze.
SEATTLE PRODUCER PARALLEL. Both Seattle electronic producers at intimate scale. Erik Blood is experimental. Gold Casio is retro-focused. Different relationships to tradition.
